novel_features.name: One-call situational brief
novel_features.description: One command returns active storms, the tropical outlook, and live NWS tropical alerts as a single payload, with an optional human briefing.
novel_features.rationale: Only a local compound command can stitch CurrentStorms.json, the Tropical Weather Outlook, and api.weather.gov alerts into one situational answer an agent can act on.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first when an agent asks 'what is the tropical situation right now' — one call instead of four.

novel_features.name: Flattened storm detail with null-safe link-outs
novel_features.description: Full detail for one storm: vitals plus every advisory-product URL and GIS link-out, with not-applicable fields returned as explicit null.
novel_features.rationale: The agent never has to re-derive AL092024_..._CONE.kmz patterns or guess which products exist; the CLI surfaces exactly what NHC published this cycle.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you have a storm id and need its products and graphics without parsing the raw feed.

novel_features.name: Parsed advisory text (TCP/TCD/TCM)
novel_features.description: Fetches and parses the Public Advisory, Forecast Discussion, or Forecast/Marine Advisory into structured fields plus the clean body text, with no HTML scraping by the agent.
novel_features.rationale: Each product has a different layout (TCP is MPH dot-delimited, TCM is KT space-delimited all-caps); product-aware extractors turn them into structured data a model can reason over.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you need the meteorologist's reasoning or exact wind/pressure/movement fields, not a web page.

novel_features.name: Tropical Weather Outlook with formation odds
novel_features.description: Parses the Tropical Weather Outlook for any basin into development areas and 48-hour / 7-day formation chances, with graphic link-outs.
novel_features.rationale: The outlook is the always-on value carrier: it answers 'is anything brewing' even when zero storms are named.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in the quiet season or to forecast-ahead; it is useful when 'storms list' is empty.

novel_features.name: Storm graphics link-outs with download/open
novel_features.description: Returns the cone, track, surge, and wind link-outs for a storm; --download saves the files locally and --open views them.
novel_features.rationale: Agent-native by default (URLs only), with optional local fetch for humans who want the actual cone or surge image.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to hand a dashboard or person the official NHC graphics without re-deriving file paths.

novel_features.name: ArcGIS MapServer layer link-outs
novel_features.description: Maps a storm to its ArcGIS REST layer URLs (forecast cone, wind field, watch/warning) for mapping clients. Link-out only; never ingested.
novel_features.rationale: Honors the choice to stay text-first while still pointing power users at the live GIS for polygons.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when a caller wants the spatial layers; this CLI references them instead of parsing them.

novel_features.name: Credits and safety disclaimer
novel_features.description: Thanks the people of the National Hurricane Center and states plainly that this is an unofficial tool and NHC/NWS is authoritative.
novel_features.rationale: Credibility and gratitude are the point of a tool about keeping people safe.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Read or surface this so users know the source of truth and who to thank.

narrative.headline: Credible, real-time National Hurricane Center data for AI agents: active storms, parsed advisories, and tropical outlooks, text-first with link-outs for everything else.
narrative.value_prop: nhc-pp-cli gives agents the most credible real-time hurricane information straight from the National Hurricane Center and the National Weather Service: active storms (CurrentStorms.json), parsed Public Advisories and Forecast Discussions, the Tropical Weather Outlook with formation odds, and live tropical watches and warnings. It is built text-first and links out to the official GIS for anyone who wants polygons. Deep thanks to the forecasters, hurricane hunters, and support staff at NHC who sacrifice so much for the safety of so many. This is an unofficial tool; in an emergency, follow the official watches, warnings, and evacuation orders from NHC, the NWS, and your local authorities.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI is wired up; no API key needed.
What tropical cyclones are active right now (empty in the quiet season).
What may be developing, even when no storms are named.
One-call situational briefing across storms, outlook, and alerts.

troubleshoots.fix: A descriptive User-Agent is mandatory; nhc-pp-cli sends one automatically, so re-run rather than calling the API by hand.
That is the verified quiet-season contract (zero active storms). Run 'nhc-pp-cli outlook' to see formation chances.
Products only exist for active or recent storms; check 'nhc-pp-cli storms list' for valid ids first.

narrative.when_to_use: Use nhc-pp-cli when an AI agent or operator needs trustworthy, current tropical-cyclone information: whether a storm is active, its forecast and intensity, the meteorologist's discussion, what may develop, and which official watches and warnings are in effect. It is ideal for situational-awareness assistants, briefing generators, and disaster-response tooling that must cite an authoritative source.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to make or replace official forecasts or evacuation decisions; defer to NHC, the NWS, and local authorities.
Do not use it for non-tropical or general weather; it covers tropical cyclones only.
Do not expect rendered GIS polygons; it links out to the ArcGIS MapServer instead of parsing it.

recipes.title: Active storms as compact JSON for an agent
recipes.explanation: Returns just the high-gravity fields so an agent does not burn context on the full feed.

recipes.title: Read the forecast discussion
recipes.explanation: Fetches and parses the meteorologist's narrative for a storm id.

recipes.title: One-call situational briefing
recipes.explanation: Bundles active storms, the outlook, and tropical alerts into a single human-readable briefing.

recipes.title: Official cone and surge graphics for a storm
recipes.explanation: Hands back the official NHC link-outs without re-deriving file paths.
